← Back to Dashboard

nzbdav Documentation

Usenet virtual filesystem with WebDAV serving — stream files directly from Usenet without downloading.

Contents

  • Overview
  • Quick Start
  • Sonarr / Radarr Integration
  • Rclone Mount Setup
  • Media Player Setup
  • WebDAV Directory Structure
  • SABnzbd-Compatible API
  • REST API Reference
  • WebSocket Events
  • STRM Files
  • Settings Reference
  • Troubleshooting

Overview

nzbdav accepts NZB files (via API, web UI, or WebDAV upload), processes them to discover contained files (RAR archives, plain files), and serves the content as a virtual WebDAV filesystem. No file data is stored locally — every read streams directly from Usenet on demand.

How It Works

  1. NZB is added — via SABnzbd API, web UI upload, or PUT to /dav/nzbs/
  2. Pipeline processes — fetches first segments to detect file types, parses PAR2 for real filenames, reads RAR headers to discover contained files
  3. Virtual files appear — in /dav/content/{category}/{job}/ as streamable files
  4. On-demand streaming — when a file is read via WebDAV, articles are fetched from Usenet, decoded, and streamed in real time
Note: nzbdav only works with NZBs containing store-compressed (m0) RAR archives or plain files. Compressed RARs are not supported because decompression would require downloading the entire archive first.

Quick Start

1. Add a Usenet Server

Go to Dashboard → Servers → Add Server. Enter your provider details (host, port, SSL, credentials, connections).

2. Configure Settings

Go to Settings and set your categories (e.g. tv, movies, music).

3. Add an NZB

Either:

  • Use the Upload NZB tab in the web UI
  • Configure Sonarr/Radarr to use nzbdav as a download client (see below)
  • Use the API: POST /api?mode=addfile or GET /api?mode=addurl&name=URL
  • PUT an NZB file to /dav/nzbs/ via any WebDAV client

4. Access Files

Once processed, files appear in /dav/content/{category}/{job}/. Mount via rclone or access directly through any WebDAV client.

Sonarr / Radarr Integration

nzbdav exposes a SABnzbd-compatible API, so Sonarr and Radarr can use it as a download client.

Step 1: Add Download Client in Sonarr/Radarr

  1. Go to Settings → Download Clients → Add
  2. Select SABnzbd
  3. Configure:
    FieldValue
    Namenzbdav
    HostYour nzbdav host (e.g. 192.168.1.100)
    Port8080 (or your configured port)
    API KeyYour API key (if configured with --api-key)
    Categorytv for Sonarr, movies for Radarr
    Use SSLNo (unless you have a reverse proxy with TLS)
  4. Click Test to verify connectivity

Step 2: Mount WebDAV with rclone

Sonarr/Radarr need to see the processed files on the local filesystem. Use rclone to mount the WebDAV:

# Create rclone remote (one-time)
rclone config create nzbdav webdav \
  url=http://localhost:8080/dav \
  vendor=other

# Mount (add to systemd/cron for persistence)
rclone mount nzbdav: /mnt/nzbdav \
  --dir-cache-time 5s \
  --vfs-cache-mode off \
  --allow-other \
  --daemon

Step 3: Configure Remote Path Mapping

In Sonarr/Radarr, add a Remote Path Mapping:

FieldValue
HostYour nzbdav host
Remote Path/content/
Local Path/mnt/nzbdav/content/
How it works: When an NZB finishes processing, nzbdav reports the completed path (e.g. /content/tv/Show.S01E01/) to Sonarr via the SAB API. Sonarr uses the remote path mapping to translate this to /mnt/nzbdav/content/tv/Show.S01E01/, where it can see the files via the rclone mount.

Rclone Mount Setup

Basic Mount

rclone mount nzbdav: /mnt/nzbdav \
  --dir-cache-time 5s \
  --vfs-cache-mode off \
  --allow-other

Recommended Flags

FlagPurpose
--dir-cache-time 5sShort cache so new files appear quickly
--vfs-cache-mode offNo local caching — stream directly
--allow-otherAllow other users (e.g. Plex) to access the mount
--buffer-size 64MLarger read-ahead buffer for smoother playback
--vfs-read-chunk-size 32MRead in larger chunks

Systemd Service

# /etc/systemd/system/nzbdav-mount.service
[Unit]
Description=nzbdav rclone mount
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=notify
ExecStart=/usr/bin/rclone mount nzbdav: /mnt/nzbdav \
  --dir-cache-time 5s \
  --vfs-cache-mode off \
  --allow-other
ExecStop=/bin/fusermount -uz /mnt/nzbdav
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Media Player Setup

Plex / Emby / Jellyfin

Point your media library at the rclone mount path (e.g. /mnt/nzbdav/content/tv/). The media server will scan the directory and find video files that stream on demand from Usenet.

Direct WebDAV Access

Some players (VLC, Kodi, Infuse) can open WebDAV URLs directly:

http://nzbdav-host:8080/dav/content/movies/Movie.2024/movie.mkv

WebDAV Directory Structure

PathDescription
/Virtual filesystem root
/content/Processed files, organized by category and job name. Streamable from Usenet.
/content/{category}/Category directory (e.g. tv, movies)
/content/{category}/{job}/Job directory with extracted files
/nzbs/Queue mirror. Lists current queue items as NZB files. Upload to add, delete to remove.
/.ids/ID-based file access by UUID

SABnzbd-Compatible API

All requests go to /api with a mode query parameter. Responses are JSON.

ModeMethodDescription
addfilePOSTUpload NZB via multipart form (field: nzbfile)
addurlGETAdd NZB from URL (&name=URL)
queueGETList queue. Use &name=delete&value={nzo_id} to remove.
historyGETList history with &start=0&limit=50. Use &name=delete&value={nzo_id} to remove.
versionGETReturns {"version": "0.1.0"}
statusGETReturns status, version, paused state
get_catsGETReturns configured categories
get_configGETReturns configuration object

Example: Upload NZB

curl -X POST "http://localhost:8080/api?mode=addfile&cat=tv" \
  -F "nzbfile=@Movie.2024.nzb"

Example: Add from URL

curl "http://localhost:8080/api?mode=addurl&name=https://example.com/file.nzb&cat=movies"

REST API Reference

In addition to the SABnzbd-compatible API, nzbdav has a REST API for server and settings management. Full interactive docs available at Swagger UI.

Server Management

EndpointMethodDescription
/api/serversGETList all Usenet servers
/api/serversPOSTAdd a server (JSON body)
/api/servers/{id}PUTUpdate a server
/api/servers/{id}DELETERemove a server
/api/servers/{id}/testPOSTTest server connectivity

Settings

EndpointMethodDescription
/api/settingsGETGet all settings
/api/settingsPUTUpdate settings (JSON body)
/api/backupGETDownload database backup
/api/logsGETGet recent log lines

WebSocket Events

Connect to ws://host:port/ws for real-time updates. Events are JSON with a type field:

// Queue status change
{"type": "Queue", "queue_count": 3, "is_processing": true, "current_job": "Movie.2024"}

// History update
{"type": "History", "total_count": 47}

// Log line
{"type": "Log", "level": "INFO", "target": "queue_manager", "message": "processed successfully", "timestamp": "14:32:01"}

STRM Files

STRM files are simple text files containing a URL. Media servers (Plex, Emby, Jellyfin) can play them by fetching the URL. nzbdav can optionally generate STRM files for each video in processed content.

Setup

  1. Go to Settings
  2. Set STRM Base URL to your nzbdav WebDAV URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8080/dav)
  3. New NZBs will generate .strm files alongside video files in /content/

STRM file content example:

http://192.168.1.100:8080/dav/content/tv/Show.S01E01/episode.mkv

Settings Reference

SettingDefaultDescription
CategoriesemptyComma-separated list (e.g. tv, movies, music). Shown in upload form and reported to Sonarr/Radarr.
File BlocklistemptyGlob patterns for files to skip (e.g. *.nfo, *.txt, *.jpg)
Ensure VideotrueFail NZBs that contain no video files
Duplicate NZBincrementWhat to do when the same NZB is added again: increment (rename), reject, or replace
WebDAV Read-OnlyfalseWhen enabled, prevents deletion of files from /content/
STRM Base URLemptyBase URL for generated STRM files. Leave empty to disable STRM generation.

Troubleshooting

NZBs stuck in "Processing"

  • Check the Logs tab for errors
  • Verify your Usenet server is configured and test connectivity
  • Ensure the NZB contains store-compressed (m0) RAR files, not compressed

NZBs going straight to "Failed"

  • Check logs for NZB blob not found — this was a known bug (fixed in current version)
  • Check for UnsupportedCompressionMethod — the NZB contains compressed RARs
  • Check for ArticleNotFound — the Usenet articles have been DMCA'd or retention has expired

Files not appearing in /content/

  • Check queue and history — if the NZB completed, files should be in /content/{category}/{job}/
  • If using rclone mount, try ls /mnt/nzbdav/content/ to refresh the cache

Playback buffering or failing

  • Increase Usenet server connections (Servers → Edit → Connections)
  • If using rclone, increase --buffer-size and --vfs-read-chunk-size
  • Check that your Usenet provider has sufficient bandwidth and retention

Pool timeout errors

  • This means all connection slots are busy. Increase the connection count in server settings.
  • If you have very few connections (1-2), processing will be slower but should still work.

Dashboard · API Docs (Swagger) · OpenAPI Spec
